summary: A well‑produced matrix captures a solid but unremarkable 1976 show, highlighted by a superb Samson & Delilah and a smoking Saint Stephen while the recording is generally good with occasional mix quirks.
review: The Dave Usborne matrix does a great job smoothing over the soundboard’s uneven balance, delivering clear vocals and a pleasant overall tone despite an early mic‑level issue. Musically the concert sits in the middle of the ’76 tour – competent playing with standout moments like Samson & Delilah, It’s All Over Now and Saint Stephen, but several stretches (Wharf Rat, Dew, NFA) feel listless or average.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is average to good (≈3★) while the matrix recording is clearly above‑average (≈4★); weighted combination yields ~3.3★.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Dave Usborne’s matrix is praised as beautiful and lifts a lackluster board source; early mic‑high on vocals, occasional mix errors in the first set, but overall clear, balanced and more listenable than the SBD.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens strong with TMNS and Sugaree, but drifts in the middle; second set features a long St. Stephen jam that many find dragging, though it contains high points. Overall the concert feels mellow/uninspired for ’76.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note his snaky leads, adjacent‑fret runs on Samson & Delilah and a unique jam before NFA, highlighting his guitar work as a highlight.
band_member_comments.Drums: Reviewer mentions routinely truncating Drumz and feeling the drum segment drags.
